items.title: Conor Oberst Opens Up About Personal Struggles: “I Was Really Close To The Edge”
items.summary: Back in 2024, Bright Eyes canceled a bunch of tour dates, citing frontman Conor Oberst's vocal issues. At the time, there was plenty of speculation that Oberst was suffering from other problems as well, and he has basically confirmed that speculation. Oberst was having a hard time.

items.title: RIP Magic – “Light Over There”
items.summary: For the past year, RIP Magic — Marco Pini, Felix Bayley-Higgins, Beth Boswell-Knight and Pedro Takahashi — have been routinely dropping singles. It started with "Loot" and "Dox." Since then, the London underground electronic pop wizards started to gain traction, which led to supporting major acts like Tame Impala and LCD Soundsystem. Their January single "5words" was even produced by James Murphy.

items.title: Nicole Atkins – “Singing In The Mirror”
items.summary: The Nashville-based singer-songwriter Nicole Atkins has been making really good music for a long time. It's the kind of sharp, classicist pop music that's easy to take for granted, but you shouldn't do that. Every time I hear a new Atkins song, I'm surprised at how good it is. At this point, I shouldn't be surprised anymore. The post Nicole Atkins – “Singing In The Mirror” appeared first on Stereogum.
